name: Swanson's Oriental Broth - Chicken Peppers Cashew
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces chicken tenders, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1/2 cup red, green, and yellow bell pepper strips (thawed and dried)
1 pinch red pepper flakes or minced ginger
1/2 cup Swanson's Oriental Broth (or chicken broth)
1/4 cup low sodium soy sauce - to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and dry skinless, boneless chicken breast tenders. Cut into bite-sized pieces.
2. In a glass bowl, marinate chicken in soy sauce for no more than 20 minutes.
3. Just before cooking, coat the chicken pieces with cornstarch.
4. Heat oil in a wok until it coats well. Fry chicken in batches, shaking off excess cornstarch, and moving cooked pieces up the sides of the wok. Save empty marinade bowl for sauce.
5. When chicken is no longer pink, add peppers, garlic (crushed), red pepper flakes or minced ginger. Cook a few minutes.
6. Add mushrooms and cook 2 more minutes.
7. Season with mixed ground peppercorns.
8. Add fish sauce if desired.
9. Add cashews.
10. Using the leftover cornstarch marinade bowl, add broth, water, and additional soy sauce to dissolve the cornstarch. Make a well in the wok and pour solution into the middle. Heat until well-cooked and thickened.
11. Serve immediately over rice.
Note: If pepper strips are frozen, thaw in sieve, rinse with cold water, then blot dry between many layers of paper towels.
